Reading this report: The current state sections (Controller Actions, Risk Ranking, Predictions) show only devices with data in the last 24h scoring window — 10 of 12 devices from the asic_aging scenario. The historical analysis sections (TE Over Time, Health Score, Anomaly Timeline) show all 12 devices across the full simulation. Scenarios have different durations: baseline (30d), cooling_failure (60d), psu_degradation (90d), summer_heatwave (90d), asic_aging (180d).

Controller Actions

Tier-based controller (2.0-tier-only). Safety constraints applied: fleet_redundancy_per_model, thermal_hard_limit_80C.

Tier assignment based on mean anomaly probability in the 24h scoring window. CRITICAL = mean risk above threshold, immediate intervention recommended. WARNING = elevated risk or TE_score below 0.8. Pie chart shows distribution within the scoring window only — devices from ended scenarios are not included.

Production Safety Note: In a live MOS deployment, controller commands pass through the orchestrator's multi-voter approval system before execution (reqVotesPos: 2, reqVotesNeg: 1). Two positive votes are required to approve any write operation; a single negative vote cancels it. The commands shown here represent recommendations that would enter this approval queue — they do not execute immediately.

CodeDescriptionSeverity
P:1High temperature protection triggeredCritical

R:1Low hashrateHigh
V:1Power initialization errorCritical
V:2PSU not calibratedHigh

Trend Analysis

Rolling window trend analysis with CUSUM regime change detection. Fleet direction distribution: stable: 12. Regime changes: 12/12 devices.

TE_score Trajectory with Projections

TE_score over the last 7 days of each device's history, colored by CUSUM regime classification. Dashed extensions = 12h linear projections. Threshold lines at 0.8 (DEGRADED) and 0.6 (severe).

Trend Slope Heatmap

TE_score slope (change per hour) across four analysis windows. Green = improving, red = declining, near-zero = stable. Divergence between short and long windows (e.g. stable at 1h but declining at 7d) indicates a recent trend reversal.

Historical Analysis (Full Simulation)

Charts below show all 12 devices across the full simulation period (2026-04-02 — 2026-06-30). Scenario durations vary — shorter scenarios produce shorter lines or fewer heatmap columns.

True Efficiency Over Time

Hourly-averaged True Efficiency (J/TH) across the full simulation for all devices. Lower is better. Lines of different lengths reflect scenario durations (30d–180d). Upward trends indicate efficiency degradation.

TE Decomposition by Device

Breakdown into hardware baseline (TE_base), voltage penalty, and cooling overhead.

Average efficiency decomposition per device. Blue = baseline hardware efficiency (TE_base, naive J/TH). Orange = voltage penalty from operating above optimal V/f. Red = cooling overhead normalized to 25°C reference. Taller total bars = worse overall efficiency.

Unit note: This report uses J/TH (joules per terahash). The MOS platform convention is W/TH/s (watts per terahash per second). These are equivalent units: 1 J/TH = 1 W·s/TH = 1 W/TH/s. For example, our 15 J/TH = MOS's 15 W/TH/s.

Device Health Score

TE_score over time. Green = nominal (1.0), red = degraded (<0.8).

Daily-averaged TE_score per device. Green (1.0) = nominal health, yellow = moderate degradation, red (<0.8) = significant degradation. White/missing = idle periods or scenario ended. Devices with fewer columns had shorter simulation durations.

Failure Risk Ranking

Mean anomaly probability per device in the last 24h scoring window. Red = flagged above threshold, green = below. Only devices active in the scoring window appear here (10 of 12). Dashed line = classification threshold (0.3, recall-biased).

Predictive Model

Regression model not available. Run train_model.py to generate multi-horizon predictions.

Anomaly Timeline (Ground Truth)

Injected anomaly patterns showing onset and ramp-up. Used as training labels.

Ground-truth anomaly labels injected by the physics simulator across the full simulation. Each panel shows one anomaly type; filled regions mark active anomaly periods per device. Labels visible here were used for training — the model does not see them during scoring.

Top Predictive Features

XGBoost feature importance (gain) from the classifier. Higher bars = more influential features in split decisions. Reflects learned signal strength, not causation. The model uses 50 features total; only the top 15 are shown.
